seo_h1: Timolaus of Cyzicus on the River Maeander

seo.browser_title: Timolaus of Cyzicus on Eulogikon Greek Archive Collection
seo.meta_title: Timolaus of Cyzicus Ancient Greek Historical Works
seo.meta_description: Timolaus of Cyzicus, a 4th century BCE historian. His historical fragments survive in later sources. Free to read and download from the Greek library archive.

biography_html:
Timolaus of Cyzicus was a historian from the city of Cyzicus, a Greek colony on the Sea of Marmara. He lived during the 4th century BCE, though no specific biographical details about his life or education survive.

He is known solely for a lost history titled Persica. The work is preserved only in fragmentary citations by later authors, such as Diodorus Siculus and Athenaeus, and dealt with Persian history and customs.

Timolaus represents a minor contributor to the Greek genre of Persian history. His Persica forms part of the Hellenistic historiographical tradition that documented the Achaemenid Empire for a Greek audience, and his fragments are occasionally cited as sources for Persian matters.
